results.possibleIdentity: Sterling C. Johnson is an engineer, lawyer, and performance artist based in the San Francisco Bay Area, widely known as "The Bubblesmith." He was active as a technical professional in the late 1960s and early 1970s, during which time he served as a Lieutenant in the United States Army. Trained in industrial engineering and mathematics, Johnson began his unique artistic pursuit of "hand-blown" bubbles while in engineering school in the early 1970s, later developing it into a professional stage act that explores the physics and mathematics of soap films. He has been a resident of the San Francisco area for decades, practicing construction law in San Rafael while maintaining his career as a bubble artist. His background as a young military officer with expertise in industrial engineering and math aligns with the profiles of technical professionals who collaborated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that sought to pair engineers with artists. Johnson is recognized as the first person to walk through a bubble film and for his ability to place bubbles inside one another without tools, often performing at science-art venues like the San Francisco Exploratorium.
